title: Apple HIG — Machine Learning
summary: Apple Human Interface Guidelines page for ML-enabled experiences covering product-level considerations such as appropriate confidence thresholds, graceful degradation, privacy, correction and feedback mechanisms, and when not to use ML. The guidance is version-sensitive and scoped to Apple platforms.
lessons: Cross-check existing capability, agency, uncertainty, privacy, and feedback contracts against Apple's ML design considerations — in particular: show only high-confidence results, provide easy correction, and design graceful degradation.
Do not use the existence of an ML capability as justification for a feature; require a clear user benefit and a tested degraded-state.
Add privacy considerations for on-device vs. server-side ML as a capability disclosure requirement.
caveats: HIG guidance is version-sensitive; revalidate on major iOS/macOS releases.
Claims are scoped to Apple platforms and Core ML / Create ML context — do not apply iOS-specific ML APIs as universal web or cross-platform requirements.
Add only claims that the page actually supports; the existence of a page does not justify a new product feature.
licenseNote: Public first-party Apple developer documentation; cite and synthesize.
antiImitationNote: Transfer product-level ML design considerations (capability, privacy, correction, degradation); do not import Apple platform APIs or iOS-specific interaction patterns as cross-platform requirements.
